title: Scleromitrion tetrandrum
taxonomy: Scleromitrion tetrandrum is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is native to tropical Africa, specifically in the regions of Cameroon, Equatorial Guinea, and Gabon. The classification of Scleromitrion tetrandrum places it within the order Lamiales. Further classification includes the subfamily Thunbergioideae. It belongs to the genus Scleromitrion.

intro: Scleromitrion tetrandrum, also known as the four-veined Scleromitrion, is a small, flowering plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Scleromitrion tetrandrum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Scleromitrion tetrandrum is often prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in the form of syrup and powder to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.
